metadata.summary: Comparative genomics automation and standardization software

documentation:
# PURPOSE Bring broadscale comparative genomics to the masses. Taxonomy-agnostic, with automated data acquisition operational with prokaryote, animal, plant, and fungal genome data. Mycotools is a compilation of computational biology tools and database ([MycotoolsDB/MTDB](https://github.com/xonq/mycotools/wiki/MycotoolsDB)) software that facilitate large-scale comparative genomics. MycotoolsDB dereplicates and locally assimilates NCBI and MycoCosm (Joint Genome Institute) genomes into a database schema with uniform file curation, scalability, and automation as guiding principles. - Database initialization: `mtdb u --init ` - Database updating: `mtdb u --update` - The MycotoolsDB (MTDB) uniformly curates GenBank/MycoCosm and local `gff` formats into a consistent standard - The `.mtdb` database format is a uniform input for analyses with 100,000s to as few as a single genome - Mycotools [software suite](https://github.com/xonq/mycotools/wiki/Usage) includes modules to automate routine-complex comparative genomics, such as phylogenetic analysis


# INSTALL Please review the [wiki](https://github.com/xonq/mycotools/wiki/Install) for installation and usage information!
# CITING If Mycotools contribute to your analysis, please cite the preprint and mention the version in-line. Konkel, Z., Slot, J. C. Mycotools: An Automated and Scalable Platform for Comparative Genomics. bioRxiv 2023.09.08.556886; doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2023.09.08.556886 ---

modules.name: mycotools.acc2fa
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: mycotools.acc2fa.extract_mtdb_accs_exp
documentation: fast extraction of accession with no inner coordinates

functions.name: mycotools.acc2fa.extract_mtdb_accs
documentation:
extract MTDB accessions from fa_dict using a list `accs`; can 
extract coordinates of an accession if labeled 
`acc[START:END] | acc[START-END]`. If start > end, extract the reverse
complement of the sequence. Returns a fa_dict of the results

functions.name: mycotools.acc2fa.extractHeaders
documentation:
searches headers for "[]", which indicate coordinate-based extraction.
otherwise, just retrieves the accession from the fasta dictionary

functions.name: mycotools.acc2fa.dbmain
mycotools.acc2fa.famain
documentation: takes in accessions, fasta, and retrieves accessions

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gff.db_main

documentation: Run homology group alignment

functions.name: mycotools.db2hgs.hmmbuild_hg
documentation: Build HMMs of homology groups

documentation:
Circumscribe and output the pangenome based on a maximum missing genome
to determine the accessory and core HGs

functions.name: mycotools.db2hgs.main
documentation:
Acquire the proteome files for an inputted MycotoolsDB, use MMseqs to

modules.name: mycotools.db2microsyntree
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: mycotools.db2microsyntree.parse_orthofinder
documentation:
imports orthofinder Orthogroups.txt "hg_file". outputs several data structures:
ome_num = {ome: number}, gene2hg = {gene: og}, i2ome = [ome0, ome1, ome2]

functions.name: mycotools.db2microsyntree.compile_cds
mycotools.db2microsyntree.parse_loci
documentation: obtain a set of tuples of HG pairs {(OG0, OG1)...}

functions.name: mycotools.db2microsyntree.form_cooccur_structures
documentation:
Imports the out_dicts from parse_loci and creates index vectors that bear
the ome_num's with a given  cooccurence. e.g. cooccur_dict[(og1, og2)] = [1, 2, 3]

functions.name: mycotools.db2microsyntree.rm_invariate_sites
documentation:
Remove invariate sites to not violate assumptions of ascertainment bias
correction model. Sum the array columns and find sums equal to the number
rows. There should be no 0 invariate columns.

functions.name: mycotools.extract_mtdb.extract_unique

functions.name: mycotools.extract_mtdb.extract_tax

functions.name: mycotools.extract_mtdb.extract_ome

functions.name: mycotools.extract_mtdb.main
documentation: Python entry point for extract_mtdb

modules.name: mycotools.acc2gbk
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gbk.col_CDS
documentation:
Collect all CDS entries from a `gff` and store them into cds_dict. 
If the gene found in the CDS is in the set of `ids` then add that type 
to the CDS dict for that protein header.

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gbk.contig2gbk
documentation:
Generate a genbank string for a contig_dict, which contains a dictionary
of protein keys and list of each gff entry associated with the protein. 
ome: ome_code
row: mtdb row
contig: contig name
contig_dict: {prot: [gff_entries]}
contig_seq: contig sequence string
faa: proteome
product_searches: {prod_id: product regular expression}

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gbk.gen_gbk
documentation: manage generating the GBK for each contiguous sequence

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gbk.ome_main
documentation: Create a genbank for the whole genome

functions.name: mycotools.acc2gbk.main
documentation: Generate a genbank for each inputed gff, its associated fna, and ome

Input a GFF data structure, and accessions, then return a dictionary set

extraction parameters

modules.name: mycotools.lib.kontools
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.split_input
documentation:
Split a standard input by removing quotations, commas, etc. and
splitting a list from various bash inputs based on white space

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.parse_run_log
documentation:
Parse a log of the previous run, or create one if it doesn't exist.
Raise a KeyError if there are deviations that should fail the existing
run

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.eprint
documentation: Prints to stderr

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.vprint
documentation: Boolean print option to stdout or stderr (e)

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.gunzip
documentation: gunzips gzip_file and removes if successful

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.findExecs
documentation:
Inputs list of dependencies, `dep`, to check path.
If dependency is in exit and dependency is not in path,
then exit.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.findEnvs
documentation:
Inputs list of paths, `envs`, to check path.
If env is not in path and it is in exit, exit.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.expandEnvVar
documentation: Expands environment variables by regex substitution

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.format_path
documentation: Goal is to convert all path types to absolute path with explicit dirs

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.collect_files
documentation:
Inputs: directory path, file extension (no "."), recursivity bool
Outputs: list of files with `filetype`
If the filetype is a list, split it, else make a list of the one entry.
Parse the environment variable if applicable. Then, obtain a clean, full 
version of the input directory. Glob to obtain the filelist for each 
filetype based on whether or not it is recursive.
need to change recursive to false

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.collect_dirs
documentation:
Inputs: directory path, recursive search boolean
Outputs: list of folders
Get folders via os, recursively extend output list if True.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.dictSplit
documentation:
Inputs: a dictionary `Dict`, and an integer `factor` to split by
Outputs: a list of split dictionaries `list_dict`
Split dictionary keys into a numpy array via the factor. For each
key, append a new dictionary to the output list and populate it 
with the list of keys within that factor. The last entry will be
whatever amount is leftover.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.sys_start
documentation:
args is a sys.argv typically, or a list of arguments.
usage is the usage statement without formatting.
min_len is the minimum number of arguments that should exist.
dirs is a list of items that should be directories
files is a list of items that should be files

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.intro
documentation:
Inputs: script_name string, args_dict dictionary of arguments, 
credit string bool / path for output log
Outputs: prints an introduction, returns start_time in YYYYmmdd format
Creates a string to populate and format for the introduction using 
keys as the left-most descriptor and arguments (values) as the right
most. Optionally outputs a log according to `log` path.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.outro
documentation:
Inputs: start time string formatted YYYYmmdd, log path
Outputs: prints execution time and exits with 0 status

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.prep_output
documentation:
Inputs: output path, bool `mkdir`, bool `require_newdir`, bool cd.
Outputs: creates directory, returns None if bool inputs fail, returns 
formatted path

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.checkDep
documentation: Checks all dependencies in path from list, optional exemption set

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.file2list
documentation:
Inputs: `file_` path, separator string, column integer index
Outputs: reads file and returns a list given arguments
If the `sep` is not valid, return None, else read the file and
split each line, if `col`, split each by the delimiter, and
acquire the column index at each line. If not `col`, then 
simply split by the separator and grab the only entry.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.multisub
documentation: return [], -420

classes.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.kon_log

methods.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.kon_log.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a print class with the base index, the number of spaces
between bases, whether or not to flush after printing, and a
placeholder for a tee variable that will input the path of a log output
file to write to

methods.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.kon_log.print
documentation: Perform the basic print function

A print class designed to enable swift string formatting while moving
between scripts

classes.name: mycotools.lib.kontools.Subqueue
documentation:
Run a subqueue of args; can run commands consecutively to avoid shell
limitations while maintaining shell injection security

documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3
NEED to make login check more intuitive and easier for NCBI only

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.log_editor
documentation: opens a `log` file path to read, searches for the `ome` code followed by a whitespace character, and edits the line with `edit`

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.primaryDB
documentation:
Acquire the path of the primary database by searching $MYCODB for a file
with a basename that starts with a date string %Y%m%d.

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.db2df
documentation: Deprecated legacy Pandas implementation of MTDB import
imports database, converts into df
returns database dataframe

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.df2std
documentation: Standardized organization of database columns. DEPRECATED pandas MTDB

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.df2db
documentation: Deprecated output pandas MTDB implementation to file
imports dataframe and organizes it in accord with the `std df` format
exports as database file into `db_path`. If rescue is set to 1, save in home folder if output doesn't exist
if rescue is set to 0, do not output database if output dir does not exit

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.hit2taxonomy
documentation:
Takes a searchTerm string, queries NCBI via Entrez, obtains TaxIDs,
if there are multiple hits return an error an query the TaxID for 
taxonomy to find a fungal hit. If there is a fungal hit, create a
tax_dict and return it.
tax_dict = { taxonomicRank: rankName }

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.prepare_tax_dicts
documentation: Identify the genera that do not have higher taxonomy ascribed to them

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.query_ncbi4taxonomy
documentation:
gather taxonomy from information present in the genus column 
and query NCBI using Entrez

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.gather_taxonomy
documentation:
Gather taxonomy for an MTDB or deprecated pd MTDB by querying NCBI's
taxonomy hierarchy

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.gather_taxonomy_dataset
documentation:
Gather taxonomy for an MTDB or deprecated pd MTDB by querying NCBI's
taxonomy hierarchy

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.parse_dataset_taxonomy
documentation: Parse the dataset taxonomy list

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.read_tax
documentation: Read taxonomy from an MTDB by converting the string into a dictionary

functions.name: mycotools.lib.dbtools.assimilate_tax
documentation: assimilate taxonomy dictionary strings and append the resulting taxonomy string dicts to an inputted database
forbid a list of taxonomic classifications you are not interested in and return a new database

MycotoolsDB (mtdb) class. Designed to support high throughput "pandas-like"
interface without the overhead of pandas.

modules.name: mycotools.update_mtdb
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3
NEED reinit implementation
NEED to update introduction
NEED a verbose option
NEED revert version option (ome-by-ome/list of omes)
NEED to reference a manually curated duplicate check
NEED a prohibit option to import prohibited JGI/NCBI IDs and option to update
NEED to finish --save implementation
NEED an only one of a strain feature in config
NEED to pull failed JGI from NCBI
will require logging whatever NCBI omes directly overlap MycoCosm
NEED to remove overlap when rerunning failed genomes

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.validate_t_and_c
documentation:
Validate that user understands and accepts the conditions of
use-restricted data, and that responsibility over confirming the
desigination of use-restriction is the user's responsibility

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.add_vars
documentation: Initialize the environmental variables for MTDB

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.initDB
documentation: Initialize database in `init_dir`

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.parse_forbidden
documentation: Read the log file containing information on forbidden genomes

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.parse_dups
documentation:
Retrieve a file containing replicated genomes and ignore these.
This is important for dereplication of discrepant genus naming between NCBI
and MycoCosm due to not adhering to conserved genus naming standards and
updating relic genus names. It appears that MycoCosm will name genera by
their anamorph occassionally, and not the consensus name - though I assume
this is also to some extent present in NCBI. Ultimately, a manually curated
file is necessary for this and should be held in a central repository.

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.acq_forbid_omes
documentation:
Parse a file with forbidden ome accessions - ome codes that have been
used before and are no longer valid
def add_dups(
dup_code, dup_entry, file_path
edit = dup_code + '\t' + '\t'.join(dup_entry)
log_editor(file_path, dup_code, edit)

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.write_forbid_omes
documentation:
Add to a file of forbidden ome accessions so that these are not ever
used again, even if the codename is removed from the database

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.parse_failed
documentation:
Parse a file that stores the failed accessions and metadata of the
attempted acquisition. Return a dictionary that contains the failed
accession and its metadata.

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.parse_jgi2ncbi
documentation: Parse previously collected NCBI to JGI data to limit querying

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.parse_true_ncbi
documentation: Parse accessions considered to be unique to NCBI

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.add_true_ncbi
documentation: Add to a ledger of accessions considered to be unique to NCBI

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.add_jgi2ncbi
documentation:
Add to a ledger that seeks to associated NCBI accessions with JGI. This
is prone to failure given that the field JGI uses to supply their genome
accession is either absent from some NCBI entries, or is in a different
field

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.add_failed
documentation:
Add genomes to the failed acquisition file, including metadata on where
it was downloaded, the version attempted to download, and the date of the
run

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.dwnld_mycocosm
documentation:
Download the MycoCosm genome data spreadsheet, format to UTF-8 and
return a Pandas dataframe of the data

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.dwnld_ncbi_metadata
documentation:
Download the NCBI genome data spreadsheet for prokaryotes or
eukaryotes, and return a Pandas dataframe

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.exec_rm_overlap
documentation:
Execute the removal of JGI redundancy from NCBI genomes and create a
DataFrame that represents the overlapping genomes

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.rm_ncbi_overlap
mycotools.update_mtdb.mk_wrk_dirs
documentation: Make the download directories in the update path

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.prepare_ref_db
documentation:
Prepare MTDBs of the data in the reference MTDB that are from the two
download sources

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.internal_redundancy_check
documentation:
Check the inputted database for overlapping assembly accessions and
dereplicate, including those with different versions of the same
accession

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.read_prev_tax
documentation: Open a genus to taxonomy JSON path

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.ref_update
documentation:
Initialize/Update the primary MTDB based on a reference database
acquired external from any existing primary MTDB

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.extract_constraint_lineages
documentation:
Extract genera from NCBI and JGI Pandas dataframes 
that hit a dictionary of lineages of interest

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.taxonomy_update
documentation:
Reset the taxonomy for the entire database and overwrite the previous
tax path data to accomodate new taxonomy

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.rogue_update
documentation: Initialize/update a standalone primary MTDB

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.rm_raw_data
documentation: Remove raw data after completion

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.gen_algn_db
documentation: Generate an alignment database for the complete primary MTDB

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.check_add_mtdb
documentation: Check the original MTDB for overlapping omes and curate if necessary

functions.name: mycotools.update_mtdb.db2primary
documentation:
Finalize an update by converting the updated MTDB into the primary
MTDB

modules.name: mycotools.ncbiDwnld
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3
NEED a db check to ensure the log is relevant to the input
NEED to convert to datasets

functions.name: mycotools.ncbiDwnld.collect_assembly_accs
documentation: collects paths to download proteomes and assemblies

documentation: Run NCBI datasets to download genomes or metadata

Unzip, identify complete downloads, parse file outputs and metadata,

modules.name: mycotools.assemblyStats
documentation:
Takes a database as argument 1 and an output for the .tsv as argument 2.
Calculates basic genome statistics.
! /usr/bin/env python3
NEED to come up with more clever way of detecting mtdb

functions.name: mycotools.assemblyStats.calcMask
documentation: Calculate the percent of the sequence that is masked (lower-cased)

functions.name: mycotools.assemblyStats.sortContigs
documentation:
Imports fasta, creates a list of dicts for each contig length and its name.
Sorts the list in descending order by length

functions.name: mycotools.assemblyStats.n50l50
documentation:
Calculates contigs greater than 1000 bp, the total length excluding 1000 bp contigs.
Then caculates l50, n50, largest contig, total contigs, shortest contig, and l50%.
Returns a dictionary of the results.

modules.name: mycotools.fa2tree
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python3
NEED to implement fa2clus?
NEED to ignore non fasta inputs
NEED to work as a standalone script

functions.name: mycotools.fa2tree.run_mafft
documentation:
Call Mafft to align an inputted fasta and either output commands for
sequential execution on an HPC, or run directly through subprocess

functions.name: mycotools.fa2tree.run_clipkit
documentation: Execute ClipKIT from a complete Mafft run

functions.name: mycotools.fa2tree.run_mf
documentation:
Run ModelFinder on its own in preparation for multigene phylogeny
reconstruction

functions.name: mycotools.fa2clus.rd_usrch_distmtx
documentation:
Imports a distance matrix with each line formatted as `organism $SEP organism $SEP distance`.
- this is equivalent to the `-tabbedout` argument in `usearch -calc_distmx`. The function 
compiles a dictionary with the information and reciprocal information for each organism in each
line, then converts this dictionary of dictionaries into a pandas dataframe. As a distance matrix,
NA values are converted to maximum distance (1).

modules.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld
documentation:
PLEASE respect JGI's ping time limits. I've tuned it to respect their
unannounced limit.


NEED to remove gff v gff3 option
! /usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.jgi_login
documentation:
Login via JGI's prescribed method by creating a cookie cache and 
downloading JGI's sign-in file.

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.retrieve_xml
documentation:
Retrieve JGI xml file tree. First check if it already exists, if not then 
download it using JGI's prescribed method. Then open the xml and check for 
the common 'Portal does not exist' error. If so, report.

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.parse_xml
documentation:
Parse the XML data to obtain the file types of interest based on
predefined hashes that contain the known subdirectories associated with JGI
organization

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.handle_redirect_307
documentation:
Handle a redirection error by identifying a new file URL to download
from, or return the original if none exist

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.no_md5_checks
documentation: If there is no MD5, simply check the file has content in it

functions.name: mycotools.jgiDwnld.jgi_dwnld
documentation:
Download JGI files. For each type of file, use regular expressions to 
gather the URL from the file. Grab the md5checksum if possible from the 
xml as well. Create arbitrary values for md5 and curl_cmd. If the download file
already exists, then run an md5 checksum if an md5 value exists in the xml.

If the md5 does not match then open and check for typical errors. If those 
errors exist, obtain the alternative download URL from the xml. If the md5 
matches, pass through the rest of the function.

If there is no download md5, then check to see if the file is greater than 10 
lines as a proxy to make sure that the file isn't empty/blatantly wrong. If it 
passes this test, change the values to not enter the while loop at the end of 
the function.

The while loop following the file exists error allows for 3 attempts. If a file 
is downloaded it will check its md5 using the xml reference. If it fails, it will 
proceed via the error checking above, wait a minute, and reattempt. If there is no 
download md5 from the xml it will proceed via the error checking above as well. If it 
passes the line count check, it will exit the loop - otherwise, it will attempt to 
gather a new URL and restart after another minute wait. This is an unfortunate
circumnavigation of JGI's cryptic maximum ping / time before booting.

functions.name: mycotools.utils.gtf2gff3.grabOutput
documentation:
Inputs: orthofiller `output_path` for results
Outputs: gff_dict and fasta_dict of results
Collect the files in `output_path`, find the proteome and gtf, and make
sure there is only one possible entry. Return the dictionary versions of
both.

functions.name: mycotools.utils.gtf2gff3.intron2exon
documentation:
Inputs: gff_dict
Outputs: gff_dict with introns converted to exons
For each entry in the gff_dict, search for the gene ID. If the gene is not
in `gene_info` add the gene as a key and populate a blank intron list, 
start codon list, stop codon list, strand string, and raw list. If the
entry type is within the `gene_info` dict for the gene, then append the
list of start and stop coordinates. Append the entire entry to the raw data
key. 
Create a dictionary `intron_genes` for each gene in gene_info if there is
an intron entry. For each gene in `intron_genes` create a blank list for
`exon_coords` dict under the key `gene`. If the intron coordinates' start
codon end coordinate is greater than the start coordinate, then change the
start codon entry in `intron_genes[gene]` to have the greater value first.
Repeat for the stop codon. Then sort the intron coordinates of that gene.
Append the appropriate exon coordinates for the intron based upon strand
sense.
For each gene in the gff, if it is not in `exon_coords` then simply append
to the `new_gff`. Then add genes with new exons.
